content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 29, 2002

Thomas R. Egan
1893 Parkview Circle
Costa Mesa, CA 92627-4536

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-02-194

Dear Mr. Egan:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act ("Act").¹ Because your request is for general advice and does not relate to either an existing governmental position or a specific governmental decision, we treat it as a request for general and informal assistance only.² Please note that we do not interpret any statutes other than those in the Political Reform Act.

QUESTION

May you run for the position of city council member for the City of Costa Mesa when your wife is currently serving a term as a planning commissioner, also for the City of Costa Mesa?

_________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, sections 18000-18997. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed.)

File No. 1-02-194
Page No. 2

CONCLUSION

Nothing in the Act prohibits you from seeking or holding an office in the same
city for which your wife is a planning commissioner. Although the Act's conflict-of-
interest laws prohibit a public official from making, participating in making, or using her
or his official position to influence decisions which may result in certain personnel
actions regarding the spouse, the mere fact that your wife already sits on the board on
which you are running for a seat does not, by itself, present a conflict of interest.

FACTS

You intend to run for election to the Costa Mesa City Council. Your wife is
currently serving as a planning commissioner for the City of Costa Mesa, a position to
which she was appointed last year by the present city council.

ANALYSIS

The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials will perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests.
(Section 87100(b).) Specifically, section 87100 prohibits any public official from
making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official position to
influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. To
decide whether or not an official has a disqualifying conflict of interest, the Commission
has adopted a standard analysis (regulation 18700 (b)(1) - (8)) discussed in the enclosed
pamphlet entitled, "Can I Vote?" The general rule, however, is that a conflict of interest
may occur whenever a public official makes a governmental decision that may affect one
or more of her or his financial interests.

The financial interest most pertinent to your question would be your community
property interest in the income of your spouse. A public official's community property
interest in a spouse's income is considered income to the public official. (Section
82030(a).) This means that the public official has an economic interest in the source of
that income and may lead to the public official being potentially disqualified from
making decisions regarding the source of the income. (Sections 87100 and 87103(c).)
However, because salary and reimbursement for expenses from a state, local, or federal
governmental agency are expressly excluded from the Act's definition of "income"
(section 82030(b)(2)), as a general rule, your wife's position as a planning commissioner
would not in and of itself create a conflict of interest for you or for her. We enclose
regulation 18707.5(b), which provides further guidance on these issues. You may want
to request further advice if you are elected.

File No. 1-02-194
Page No. 3

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Adrianne Korchmaros
Political Reform Consultant
